description: C4 model for the Magrathea S3-compatible object storage service

children.description: One Java process owns the S3 composition, one Ratis voter, and one independent replica data server. Its stable UUID is recovered from the identity root; consensus, object, temporary, and runtime roots are independent. Internal peers authenticate the UUID through configured mutual TLS trust.
children.name: Node A JVM

children.description: One Java process owns the S3 composition, one Ratis voter, and one independent replica data server. Its stable UUID and persisted roots survive complete restart; B serves failover reads with C retaining quorum after A stops.
children.name: Node B JVM

children.description: One Java process owns the S3 composition, one Ratis voter, and one independent replica data server. Its stable UUID and persisted roots survive complete restart; C retains control quorum with B after A stops.
children.name: Node C JVM

deploymentNodes.description: Bounded implemented topology: fixed nodes A, B, and C are placed in distinct available failure domains. This baseline is not a production topology recommendation. Production certificate enrollment, custody, rotation, revocation, expiry monitoring, recovery, and broader operational readiness remain outside the slice.
deploymentNodes.name: Independent failure domains

children.description: Initial S3 coordinator and co-located voter/storage node; stopped after successful CreateBucket and whole-object PUT.
children.name: Acceptance node A

children.description: Surviving S3 endpoint and co-located voter/storage node used for exact-byte GET and post-restart recovery.
children.name: Acceptance node B

children.description: Surviving co-located voter/storage node that retains control quorum with B and recovers persisted state after complete restart.
children.name: Acceptance node C

deploymentNodes.description: Validated test topology starts fixed A/B/C as real child JVM processes with separate roots, ephemeral test-local CA material, per-node certificates, and test-only trust stores. The fixtures exercise mTLS locally; they are not production PKI or production-readiness evidence.
deploymentNodes.name: Isolated acceptance environment

components.description: In-memory reactive implementation of BucketCommandRepository and BucketQueryRepository. Internally stores bucket aggregates in ConcurrentHashMap structures. Extended to support session, ABAC, object lock, and metadata/table configuration storage.
components.name: InMemoryReactiveBucketRepository

components.description: In-memory reactive implementation of S3ObjectCommandRepository and S3ObjectQueryRepository. Internally stores object metadata and raw object bytes in ConcurrentHashMap structures. Extended to support legal hold, retention, restore, and select request/response storage.

components.description: In-memory reactive implementation of MultipartUploadCommandRepository and MultipartUploadQueryRepository. Internally stores multipart upload sessions in a ConcurrentHashMap structure.

components.description: Pure domain model: StoragePolicy, EffectiveStoragePolicy, VirtualDevice, DedupNamespace, WorkflowCompatibilityKey, DeviceConfigurationHash, StepPlan, StepExecutionTrace, ChunkPersistenceTrace, ObjectManifest, StorageDevice, DiskSet, and StoredObject aggregate. Zero framework dependencies. MINIO_STANDARD semantics are STANDARD with dedup disabled, EC enabled (4 data / 2 parity), replication factor 1, compression disabled, and encryption disabled by default.
components.name: storage-engine-domain

components.description: Reactive ports, catalogs, and repository interfaces used by the Storage Engine application: StoragePolicyCatalog, StorageDeviceCatalog, DiskSetCatalog, StoredObjectRepository, ObjectManifestRepository, ContentAddressIndex, DataTransformPort, AlterationPort, ChunkStorePort, and ECOutcome. SHA-256 checksums are computed directly inside pipeline/filesystem components without a dedicated checksum abstraction.
components.name: storage-engine-reactive-repository-application

relationships.description: Implemented by filesystem and YAML adapters

components.description: Reactive orchestration and use cases: ReactiveStorageOrchestrator, Chunker, and ApplicationChunkPayload coordinate domain planning with repository/catalog/adapter ports.
components.name: storage-engine-reactive-application

relationships.description: Uses domain services (PersistencePlanner, EffectivePolicyResolver, VirtualDeviceResolver, CompleteUploadService)
Uses ports and catalogs (ChunkStorePort, ContentAddressIndex, ObjectManifestRepository, StoredObjectRepository, StoragePolicyCatalog, StorageDeviceCatalog, DiskSetCatalog)

components.description: Filesystem and YAML adapters/configuration: FileSystemStorageCluster, FileSystemStorageNode, FileSystemVirtualDeviceMapper, FileSystemContentAddressIndex, FileSystemManifestRepository, FileSystemStoredObjectRepository, YAML policy/device/disk-set catalogs, compression/encryption/EC/replication adapters, direct SHA-256 calculation in filesystem/pipeline components, and FaultInjectingStorageCluster chaos decorator. MINIO_STANDARD YAML enables EC (4 data / 2 parity) and disables dedup; current C4 docs do not claim verified physical EC shard placement.
components.name: storage-engine-reactive-infrastructure

components.description: Anti-Corruption Layer + adapter: implements Object Store repository interfaces using the Storage Engine backend. Contains ObjectStoreToStorageEngineTranslator, StorageEngineReactiveS3ObjectRepository, StorageEngineReactiveBucketRepository, StorageEngineReactiveMultipartUploadRepository.
components.name: object-store-reactive-repository-storage-engine-infrastructure

relationships.description: Delegates persistence to Storage Engine orchestrator
For the cluster profile, delegates unconditional CreateBucket and whole-object PUT/GET through transport-neutral cluster ports; remains the sole S3-to-Storage-Engine ACL
For the cluster profile, delegates unconditional CreateBucket and whole-object PUT/GET through transport-neutral cluster ports; remains the sole S3-to-Storage-Engine ACL

containers.description: Storage Engine bounded context: persistence pipeline, virtual devices, storage policies, optional deduplication, erasure coding, content-addressed storage, and manifest management. MINIO_STANDARD is modeled as STANDARD with dedup disabled, EC enabled (4 data / 2 parity), replication factor 1, compression disabled, and encryption disabled by default.
containers.name: storage-engine

relationships.description: For the cluster profile, delegates unconditional CreateBucket and whole-object PUT/GET through transport-neutral cluster ports; remains the sole S3-to-Storage-Engine ACL
For the cluster profile, delegates unconditional CreateBucket and whole-object PUT/GET through transport-neutral cluster ports; remains the sole S3-to-Storage-Engine ACL

components.description: Implemented transport-neutral CreateBucket, whole-object write/read coordination, PA-6 placement, authoritative metadata ports, local-artifact ports, acknowledgement fencing, and replica-transfer ports. Reactor-facing where needed; contains no Ratis, protobuf, generated-stub, or gRPC types.
components.name: storage-engine-cluster-application

relationships.description: Invokes pure PA-6 placement for fixed N=3/W=2 without adding transport or lifecycle types to storage-engine-domain
Invokes pure PA-6 placement for fixed N=3/W=2 without adding transport or lifecycle types to storage-engine-domain
Resolves and commits bucket/object-reference generations through the fixed three-voter control group
Stages, sends, or reads immutable whole-object artifacts and requires checksum-valid durable acknowledgements
PLANNED: extend the bounded object coordinator beyond unconditional whole-object PUT/GET
PLANNED: coordinate policy-specific shard placement, transfer, acknowledgement, and reconstruction
PLANNED: execute durable fenced healing, rebalance, and cleanup work

components.description: Implemented versioned internal protobuf contracts and generated messages/stubs for health and immutable-artifact stage/read transfer. It contains no S3 facade, Spring application behavior, storage-policy decisions, or domain logic.
components.name: cluster-protocol

components.description: Implemented embedded Ratis lifecycle, deterministic bucket/object-reference generation state machine, fixed A/B/C bootstrap manifest, stable UUID identity recovery, persisted log/snapshots, and identity-bound control mTLS. Dynamic membership is future scope.
components.name: cluster-control-ratis-infrastructure

relationships.description: A/B/C voters commit bucket and object-reference generations; peers require stable-UUID-bound mTLS
PLANNED: commit safe membership and lifecycle transitions instead of editing fixed bootstrap
PLANNED: extend and validate control behavior beyond one stopped coordinator

components.description: Implemented independent replica gRPC server/clients, peer identity checks, bounded 64 KiB framing and manual flow control, deadlines/cancellation, checksum validation, durable local artifacts, and replica acknowledgements.
components.name: cluster-data-grpc-infrastructure

relationships.description: Uses versioned replica transfer contracts with bounded manual flow control
Selected A/B/C storage roles transfer whole-object replicas directly and return durable checksum-valid acknowledgements; peers require stable-UUID-bound mTLS
PLANNED: extend and validate data-path behavior under broader partitions and message faults

components.description: PLANNED / NOT IMPLEMENTED: clustered multipart state and part transfer, conditional writes, S3 versioning, and chunked-object transfer. Existing single-node behavior is not cluster evidence.
components.name: Future clustered object semantics

components.description: PLANNED / NOT IMPLEMENTED: encode and transfer data/parity shards, apply policy-specific acknowledgement thresholds, and reconstruct reads. Whole-object N=3/W=2 replication is not erasure-coding evidence.
components.name: Future erasure-coded transfer
components.technology: Future cluster application and data-plane capability

components.description: PLANNED / NOT IMPLEMENTED: consensus-controlled admission, catch-up, promotion, demotion, replacement, removal, fencing, certificate lifecycle, and rolling compatibility. Static A/B/C bootstrap is not dynamic membership.
components.name: Future dynamic membership lifecycle

components.description: PLANNED / NOT IMPLEMENTED: durable anti-entropy, healing, rebalance, and orphan-cleanup jobs. Existing planning models do not execute repair or movement.
components.name: Future healing and rebalance execution

components.description: PLANNED / NOT VALIDATED: behavior for asymmetric partitions, delayed or reordered messages, split control/data paths, stale leaders, duplicate identities, and repeated coordinator changes. The validated baseline covers only one stopped coordinator while the surviving peers remain mutually reachable.
components.name: Future broader partition handling

containers.description: Implemented and validated only for the bounded EP-10 first slice: one co-located voter/storage runtime, instantiated as fixed nodes A, B, and C. Each JVM owns one Ratis voter and one independent replica data server. Consensus orders bucket and immutable whole-object-reference generations; direct N=3/W=2 replication has no degraded writes. Internal only; not an object API and not a production-readiness claim.
containers.name: Cluster Node Runtime

relationships.description: Invokes pure PA-6 placement for fixed N=3/W=2 without adding transport or lifecycle types to storage-engine-domain
Invokes pure PA-6 placement for fixed N=3/W=2 without adding transport or lifecycle types to storage-engine-domain

containers.technology: Java 21, Apache Ratis 3.2.2, grpc-java 1.82.2, protobuf 3.25.8, mTLS

softwareSystems.description: AWS S3-compatible object storage and bundled admin/documentation UI built with Spring Boot 4 WebFlux and Java 21.
softwareSystems.name: Magrathea ObjectStore

name: Magrathea ObjectStore

componentViews.description: Executable application assembly and runtime composition boundary. It starts the S3 WebFlux application, starts the admin/static Netty server, selects the object-store backend, and serves static UI/docs/report assets copied into bootstrap-application during generate-resources. Static assets are no longer owned by admin-api-adapter.
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - bootstrap-application
componentViews.title: C3 Component: bootstrap-application

componentViews.description: Implemented EP-7 frontend boundaries. The deployable application composes the product-neutral Product Shell and independently maintained Object Storage Product Extension, owns browser/platform and documentation integration, and injects separate typed Admin API and optional S3 HeadObject clients. Operational report screens preserve provider-not-configured responses; no absent report, audit, metrics, trace, recovery, garbage-collection, or scrub provider is modeled.
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - Object Storage Admin Browser Application
componentViews.title: C3 Component: Object Storage Admin Browser Application

componentViews.description: Implemented JSON Admin Control Plane routes consumed by the browser application and mounted by the bootstrap admin server. Catalog and capacity behavior is backed by configured ports; operational reports explicitly remain unavailable when no matching real provider is configured. Frontend and generated documentation assets are handed off to bootstrap-application static resources.
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - admin-api-adapter
componentViews.title: C3 Component: admin-api-adapter

componentViews.description: RouterFunction and handler components that expose the S3-compatible HTTP protocol. Includes S3ProxyRouter, S3SessionHandler, S3WebSupport, and all six handler components with registry dispatch in S3BucketConfigHandler.
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - s3-reactive-api-adapter
componentViews.title: C3 Component: s3-reactive-api-adapter

componentViews.description: Reactive object and multipart upload application services, domain aggregates with state transitions and domain events, repository ports, and content boundary. Supports Phase F advanced operations (legal hold, retention, torrent, restore, select, Object Lambda, encryption update).
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - reactive-object-store
componentViews.title: C3 Component: reactive-object-store

componentViews.description: Reactive bucket management service, bucket aggregate root with dedicated with* methods per config type and specific ObjectStoreEvent subtypes, and repository port. Supports Phase F advanced bucket config (ABAC, object lock, metadata/table config).
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - reactive-bucket-management
componentViews.title: C3 Component: reactive-bucket-management

componentViews.description: Command/Query repository interfaces for bucket, S3Object and multipart upload aggregates.
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - reactive-repository-application
componentViews.title: C3 Component: reactive-repository-application

componentViews.description: Reactive repository adapters used by the application capabilities. Extended to support session, ABAC, object lock, metadata/table configuration, legal hold, retention, restore, and select data. Internal ConcurrentHashMap structures are intentionally not shown as C4 components.
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - reactive-infrastructure
componentViews.title: C3 Component: reactive-infrastructure

componentViews.description: Storage Engine bounded context: domain model, reactive repository/application ports, reactive orchestration layer, filesystem/YAML adapters, and Anti-Corruption Layer adapter that implements Object Store repository interfaces using the Storage Engine backend. The existing adapter remains the only S3 integration boundary. PA-6 storage-engine-domain remains pure; the bounded EP-10 cluster modules stay outside this container and call its transport-free placement model.
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - storage-engine
componentViews.title: C3 Component: storage-engine

componentViews.description: IMPLEMENTED AND VALIDATED FOR THE BOUNDED FIRST SLICE ONLY. The node runtime contains transport-neutral cluster application code, versioned replica protocol, one embedded Ratis voter, and one independent grpc-java data server per JVM. Fixed A/B/C bootstrap, stable UUID-backed roots, identity-bound mTLS, N=3/W=2 whole-object replication, consensus publication, one-coordinator failover, and complete restart are in scope. This diagram excludes later capabilities and does not claim production readiness.
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - Cluster Node Runtime
componentViews.title: C3 Component: Bounded First-Slice Cluster Node Runtime

componentViews.description: DISTINCT FUTURE SCOPE, NOT PART OF THE VALIDATED FIRST SLICE. Clustered multipart and conditional/versioned writes, erasure-coded transfer/reconstruction, dynamic membership and certificate lifecycle, durable healing/rebalance/orphan cleanup, and broader partition behavior remain planned or unvalidated. Single-node features and PA-6 planning models do not upgrade these cluster capabilities.
componentViews.name: Component View: Magrathea ObjectStore - Cluster Node Runtime
componentViews.title: C3 Component: Future Cluster Capabilities — Planned / Not Implemented

containerViews.description: Implemented boundaries remain unchanged: bootstrap-application assembles the S3 and Admin runtimes, the browser uses only those established interfaces, and storage-engine remains behind the Object Store ACL. ADR 0028 records the bounded implementation-informed EP-10 baseline: fixed co-located voter/storage nodes A/B/C for unconditional CreateBucket and whole-object PUT/GET. Each JVM owns one Ratis voter and one replica data server. Consensus orders bucket/object-reference generations; direct N=3/W=2 replica transfer has no degraded writes. Stable UUID identities and roots survive complete restart. Internal links use identity-bound mTLS. Fixed bootstrap is not dynamic membership, and this slice is not distributed production readiness.
containerViews.name: Container View: Magrathea ObjectStore
containerViews.title: C2 Container: Magrathea ObjectStore

deploymentViews.description: IMPLEMENTED FIRST-SLICE TOPOLOGY; NOT A PRODUCTION-READINESS CLAIM. Three co-located voter/storage JVMs use an identical static manifest, stable UUID identities, and independent identity, Ratis, object, temporary, and runtime roots. Each JVM owns one Ratis voter and one replica data server. Control and direct replica links require identity-bound mutual TLS. Fixed bootstrap supports no add, promote, demote, replace, or remove operation; production PKI and operational readiness remain outside this slice.
deploymentViews.name: Deployment View: Magrathea ObjectStore - EP-10 Fixed Three-Node Runtime Baseline
deploymentViews.title: Deployment: Bounded Fixed A/B/C Runtime Baseline

deploymentViews.description: IMPLEMENTED AND VALIDATED FOR THE BOUNDED TEST TOPOLOGY. Real child JVMs run isolated A/B/C roots with ephemeral test-local CA/certificate/trust fixtures. After CreateBucket and N=3/W=2 whole-object PUT through A, A stops and exact bytes are read through B while B+C retain quorum. A later complete stop/restart recovers stable identities, committed control state, and object bytes. Test certificates are not production identity, enrollment, rotation, revocation, recovery, or readiness evidence.
deploymentViews.name: Deployment View: Magrathea ObjectStore - EP-10 Real-Process Acceptance Baseline
deploymentViews.title: Deployment: Validated Real-Process A/B/C Acceptance Baseline

dynamicViews.description: The admin server delivers the independently built browser distribution and bundled documentation. The running browser application composes Product Shell with the Object Storage Product Extension and calls implemented Admin Control Plane routes. Operational report routes return provider-not-configured when no real provider exists; S3 HeadObject is attempted only when its separate endpoint and signer are configured.
dynamicViews.name: Dynamic View: Magrathea ObjectStore - Object Storage Admin Browser Application

relationships.description: 1. GET /, /assets/**, or /docs/**
2. Resolve classpath static resource
3. Deliver application and documentation assets
4. Navigate an administration screen
5. Render the registered product route
6. Request Admin Control Plane data
7. GET/POST/PUT /admin/**

dynamicViews.title: Runtime: Object Storage Administration

dynamicViews.description: IMPLEMENTED AND VALIDATED FOR THE FIRST SLICE. The existing S3 and Object Store boundaries submit an unconditional CreateBucket through the Storage Engine ACL. The fixed A/B/C voter group commits the new bucket generation before S3 success. Dynamic membership and broader bucket configuration are outside this slice.
dynamicViews.name: Dynamic View: Magrathea ObjectStore - s3-reactive-api-adapter

relationships.description: 1. Submit unconditional S3 CreateBucket through node A
2. Invoke existing bucket use case
3. Persist through the existing repository boundary
4. Select the cluster-profile repository
5. Enter the existing Storage Engine ACL
6. Submit the transport-neutral CreateBucket command
7. Commit the next bucket generation through A/B/C quorum
8. Replicate and commit the generation before success

dynamicViews.title: Runtime: Bounded Fixed-Cluster CreateBucket

dynamicViews.description: IMPLEMENTED AND VALIDATED FOR THE FIRST SLICE. Node A coordinates one unconditional, single-part whole-object PUT. PA-6 selects fixed storage nodes A/B/C for N=3; direct replica transfer must obtain W=2 checksum-valid durable acknowledgements, then the A/B/C control quorum commits the object-reference generation before S3 success. There is no degraded write: fewer than two data acknowledgements or loss of control quorum fails publication.
dynamicViews.name: Dynamic View: Magrathea ObjectStore - s3-reactive-api-adapter

relationships.description: 1. Submit unconditional whole-object S3 PutObject through node A
2. Invoke existing object write use case
3. Persist through the existing repository boundary
4. Select the cluster-profile repository
5. Enter the existing Storage Engine ACL
6. Coordinate the transport-neutral whole-object write
7. Use pure PA-6 placement to select A/B/C at N=3/W=2
8. Stage and directly transfer the immutable whole-object artifact
9. Return identity-bound checksum-valid durable acknowledgements from selected nodes
10. Only after W=2, submit the verified object-reference generation
11. Commit the reference through A/B/C quorum before S3 success

dynamicViews.title: Runtime: Bounded Fixed-Cluster Whole-Object Write

dynamicViews.description: IMPLEMENTED AND VALIDATED FOR THE FIRST-SLICE FAILOVER PROOF. After a successful whole-object PUT, coordinator A is stopped. B and C retain the two-voter control quorum. A client uses node B's existing S3 endpoint; B resolves the committed object-reference generation and returns bytes from a referenced checksum-valid replica. This proves only the narrow one-node-stop slice, not dynamic membership, healing, rebalance, broader partitions, upgrades, or production readiness.
dynamicViews.name: Dynamic View: Magrathea ObjectStore - s3-reactive-api-adapter

relationships.description: 1. After stopping A, submit S3 GetObject through node B
2. Invoke existing object read use case
3. Read through the existing repository boundary
4. Select the cluster-profile repository
5. Enter the existing Storage Engine ACL on B
6. Coordinate the transport-neutral whole-object read
7. B resolves the committed reference while B+C retain quorum
8. B+C serve the authoritative committed generation; A is unavailable
9. Read a referenced whole-object replica and validate checksum and length
10. Stream exact bytes to B from a surviving referenced replica when needed

dynamicViews.title: Runtime: Exact-Byte GET After Coordinator A Stops

dynamicViews.description: IMPLEMENTED AND VALIDATED FOR THE FIRST-SLICE RESTART PROOF. All three JVMs stop and discard process memory, then restart from their original non-empty roots. Each node recovers its stable UUID, Ratis state, immutable replicas, and committed bucket/object-reference generations. Reordered seeds do not rewrite persisted membership. An exact-byte GET through B verifies recovery; rolling upgrade and membership change are outside this proof.
dynamicViews.name: Dynamic View: Magrathea ObjectStore - s3-reactive-api-adapter

relationships.description: 1. Before restart, address a consensus-committed whole object through S3
2. Stop all A/B/C voters after persisted log and snapshots
3. Stop all A/B/C replica servers with published artifacts on independent roots
4. Restart each voter from its stable UUID and non-empty Ratis root
5. Restart each replica server from its object root
6. Node B requests the committed object through the cluster profile
7. Resolve recovered bucket and object-reference generations
8. Validate length and checksum and return exact bytes

dynamicViews.title: Runtime: Complete A/B/C Restart Recovery

5. Persist Bucket aggregate

dynamicViews.description: GET/PUT/DELETE /{bucket}?{config} dispatches to S3BucketConfigHandler which uses registry/strategy pattern to call ReactiveBucketService methods that update the Bucket aggregate root with dedicated with* config methods.

relationships.description: 1. GET/PUT/DELETE /{bucket}?{config}

3. Read/update Bucket aggregate root with dedicated with* config method
4. Persist Bucket aggregate via domain repository port
5. CQS command interface
6. Persist updated Bucket aggregate with configuration

dynamicViews.description: Phase F advanced operations (legal hold, retention, torrent, restore, select, Object Lambda, rename, encryption update, ABAC, object lock, session, metadata/table config) follow the established RouterFunction dispatch and service delegation pattern. Two persistence implementations exist: in-memory (reactive-infrastructure) and storage-engine profile (storage-engine).
